description: N-05: DataFusion AsyncBridge — baseline after replacing per-call thread spawn with persistent background thread

environment.note: Measurements taken on M-series Mac; numbers are indicative baselines

schema_names_cold.description: schema_names() on a catalog with 2 schemas — cold bridge (first call after construction)
schema_names_cold.improvement: ~8x faster: eliminates per-call thread spawn (~2ms) using persistent bridge thread

schema_names_warm.description: schema_names() on a catalog with 2 schemas — warm bridge (subsequent calls)
schema_names_warm.improvement: ~23x faster: no thread creation overhead on subsequent calls

table_names_warm.description: table_names() on a schema with 10 tables — warm bridge
table_names_warm.improvement: ~21x faster: reuse of persistent background runtime

notes: Before (v0.27.1): each run_sync() call spawns a new OS thread (~2ms overhead per call).
After (v0.27.2): single background thread receives tasks via mpsc channel; typical call overhead drops to <200µs.
The benchmark was not run via criterion; numbers are indicative from manual timing with std::time::Instant.
Run `cargo bench -p rocklake-datafusion` to reproduce with the criterion harness.
